title: Engineer code change for one task iteration
description: What the Engineer subagent returns after implementing (or refining) the current task. It applies the change to <repo> directly; this object is the record QA and the orchestrator read. The Engineer edits only source files — never test files — so the producer cannot move the gate it is graded against.

task_id.description: The tasks.json task id this change implements, e.g. T3.

iteration.description: Inner-loop iteration for this task (1 = first attempt).

summary.description: One paragraph: what was implemented/changed and how it advances the task's acceptance criteria.

files_changed.description: Source files touched. Must be a subset of the task's `files` (source only); editing a test file is a hard gate failure.

grounded_in.description: What this change addresses: a specific failing test name, a QA fix id, or an acceptance_criterion from the task. On iteration 1 this is the task's objective.

new_feature.description: True if this change adds behaviour beyond the task's existing tests (signals QA to author new tests rather than only grade quality).

refine_action.description: Set on refine rounds (iteration > 1). refine = accept QA's fixes and improve; pivot = current approach is a dead end, try a different one; double_down = QA is wrong, defend with justification in `note`.

note.description: Refinement reasoning, or a justification when refine_action is double_down.
